title: Ensure rsyslog is configured to send logs to a remote log host

prose.en: Ensure rsyslog is configured to send logs to a remote log host

description.en: `rsyslog` supports the ability to send log events it gathers to a remote log host or to receive messages from remote hosts, thus enabling centralized log management.

rationale.en: Storing log data on a remote host protects log integrity from local attacks. If an attacker gains root access on the local system, they could tamper with or remove log data that is stored on the local system.

audit.en:
Edit the `rsyslog` configuration and add the following line (where `loghost.example.com` is the name of your central log host). The `target` directive may either be a fully qualified domain name or an IP address.  

*Example script to create a drop\-in configuration file:*  

Run the following command to reload `rsyslog.service`:

```
# systemctl reload-or-restart rsyslog.service
```

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash

{
   a_parameters=('*.* action(type="omfwd" target="loghost.example.com" port="514" protocol="tcp"' \
   '           action.resumeRetryCount="100"' '           queue.type="LinkedList" queue.size="1000")')
   [ ! -d "/etc/rsyslog.d/" ] && mkdir /etc/rsyslod.d/
   printf '%s\n' "" "${a_parameters[@]}" >> /etc/rsyslog.d/60-rsyslog.conf
}
```

remediation.en:
Edit the `rsyslog` configuration and add the following line (where `loghost.example.com` is the name of your central log host). The `target` directive may either be a fully qualified domain name or an IP address.  

*Example script to create a drop\-in configuration file:*  

Run the following command to reload `rsyslog.service`:

```
# systemctl reload-or-restart rsyslog.service
```

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash

{
   a_parameters=('*.* action(type="omfwd" target="loghost.example.com" port="514" protocol="tcp"' \
   '           action.resumeRetryCount="100"' '           queue.type="LinkedList" queue.size="1000")')
   [ ! -d "/etc/rsyslog.d/" ] && mkdir /etc/rsyslod.d/
   printf '%s\n' "" "${a_parameters[@]}" >> /etc/rsyslog.d/60-rsyslog.conf
}
```

references: See the rsyslog.conf(5) man page for more information.
